name: Paracetamol
description: Reduces fever and relieves mild to moderate pain.
mechanismOfAction: Acts in the central nervous system by inhibiting COX enzymes, lowering prostaglandins to reduce pain and fever.

dosage.adults: 500–1000 mg every 4–6 hours. Max: 4000 mg (4g) per day.
dosage.children: 10–15 mg/kg every 6 hours based on weight (doctor consultation recommended).

warnings: Not suitable for people with liver or kidney disease
Do not exceed the recommended dose
Call doctor if pain/fever lasts more than 3 days
precautions: Avoid alcohol. Not suitable for people with liver or kidney disease. Do not exceed the recommended dose.
storage: Store below 25°C in a cool, dry place. Keep out of reach of children.
clinicalTrials: Extensively tested for safety; part of WHO Essential Medicines List. Gentler on the stomach than NSAIDs.

name: Ibuprofen
description: Used to relieve pain and reduce inflammation.
mechanismOfAction: Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) that inhibits prostaglandin synthesis.

dosage.adults: 200-400 mg every 4-6 hours as needed. Max 1200mg/day (OTC).
dosage.children: Based on weight (approx 10mg/kg). Consult a pediatrician.

warnings: Avoid in patients with peptic ulcers or GI bleeding
Caution in kidney or heart conditions
Do not combine with alcohol or other NSAIDs
Take with food or milk to reduce stomach upset
precautions: Risk of stomach bleeding. Avoid alcohol.
storage: Store at room temperature away from moisture and heat.
clinicalTrials: Commonly used since the 1960s; widely considered safe for short-term pain relief.

name: Amoxicillin
description: A penicillin antibiotic used to treat many bacterial infections like tonsillitis, pneumonia, and ear infections.
mechanismOfAction: Bactericidal; inhibits the synthesis of bacterial cell walls by binding to penicillin-binding proteins.

dosage.adults: 250mg to 500mg every 8 hours or 500mg to 875mg every 12 hours.
dosage.children: 20-40 mg/kg/day in divided doses depending on the severity of infection.

precautions: Do not use if allergic to penicillin. Complete the entire prescribed course even if symptoms resolve.
storage: Store capsules at room temperature. Oral suspension should be refrigerated and discarded after 14 days.
clinicalTrials: Solid track record for pediatric and adult respiratory infections; part of main-stream antibiotic therapy.

name: Metformin
description: Used to control high blood sugar in patients with type 2 diabetes along with diet and exercise.
mechanismOfAction: Decreases hepatic glucose production, decreases intestinal absorption of glucose, and improves insulin sensitivity.

dosage.adults: Initially 500mg twice daily or 850mg once daily with meals.
dosage.children: 10 years and older: Initially 500mg twice daily.

precautions: Risk of lactic acidosis. Not for patients with severe kidney disease. Monitor blood glucose regularly.
storage: Store between 20°C to 25°C. Protect from light.
clinicalTrials: Extensively studied and proven to reduce cardiovascular mortality in type 2 diabetic patients.

name: Atorvastatin (Lipitor)
description: Lowering bad cholesterol and fats (triglycerides) and raising good cholesterol (HDL) in the blood.
mechanismOfAction: Selective, competitive inhibitor of HMG-CoA reductase, the rate-limiting enzyme that converts HMG-CoA to mevalonate.

dosage.adults: 10 to 80mg once daily, any time of day, with or without food.
dosage.children: 10-20mg once daily (10-17 years old) for specific genetic conditions.

precautions: Avoid during pregnancy. Limit alcohol intake. Report any unexplained muscle pain to your doctor immediately.
clinicalTrials: Major trials (ASCOT, CARDS) demonstrated significant reduction in cardiovascular events.

name: Lisinopril
description: Used to treat high blood pressure (hypertension) and heart failure. Often used after a heart attack to improve survival.
mechanismOfAction: Angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitor that reduces levels of angiotensin II, leading to vasodilation.

dosage.adults: Hypertension: 10mg daily. Heart Failure: 5-10mg daily. Max 40-80mg daily.
dosage.children: Based on body weight for children 6 years and older.

precautions: May cause high blood potassium levels. Avoid use in pregnancy. Monitor kidney function during therapy.
storage: Store in a cool dry place, protected from moisture.
clinicalTrials: Standard treatment for hypertension since the 1980s with strong safety profile.

name: Omeprazole
description: Used to treat acid reflux (GERD), stomach ulcers, and conditions where the stomach produces too much acid.
mechanismOfAction: Suppresses gastric acid secretion by specific inhibition of the H+/K+-ATPase enzyme system at the secretory surface of the gastric parietal cell.

dosage.adults: 20mg to 40mg once daily taken 30-60 minutes before a meal.
dosage.children: Dosage based on weight for children over 1 year of age.

precautions: Prolonged use may lead to bone fractures or Vitamin B12 deficiency. Do not crush or chew capsules.
storage: Store at room temperature in a tightly closed container.
clinicalTrials: Well-established as a potent long-term treatment for erosive esophagitis.

name: Levothyroxine
description: Used to replace thyroxine, a hormone that is normally produced by your thyroid gland to regulate the body's energy and metabolism.
mechanismOfAction: Synthetically replaces the natural L-thyroxine (T4) hormone which is converted to T3 in the body.

dosage.adults: Typically 1.6 mcg/kg/day, adjusted based on TSH lab results.
dosage.children: Requires careful titration based on growth and weight.

precautions: Take on an empty stomach 30-60 minutes before breakfast. Do not switch brands without doctor consultation.
storage: Protect from light and moisture. Store at room temperature.
clinicalTrials: Gold standard therapy for hypothyroidism since the mid-20th century.

name: Albuterol Inhaler
description: Rescue inhaler used for the relief and prevention of bronchospasm in patients with asthma or exercise-induced bronchospasm.
mechanismOfAction: Beta-2 adrenergic agonist that relaxes bronchial smooth muscle, improving airflow to the lungs.

dosage.adults: 1 to 2 inhalations every 4 to 6 hours as needed for shortness of breath.
dosage.children: 1 to 2 inhalations every 4 to 6 hours for children 4 years and older.

precautions: Rinse mouth after use. If using more than twice a week, consult a physician about maintenance therapy.
storage: Store canister with the mouthpiece down. Keep away from heat and open flame.
clinicalTrials: Considered a highly effective 'rescue' medication for acute asthma symptoms globally.

name: Loratadine
description: Non-drowsy antihistamine that treats symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes caused by hay fever or allergies.
mechanismOfAction: Long-acting, non-sedating, peripheral histamine H1-receptor antagonist.

dosage.children: Children 2-6 years: 5mg once daily. 6+ years: 10mg once daily.

precautions: Consult doctor if you have kidney or liver disease. Usually safe but monitor for allergic reactions.
storage: Store in a dry place at room temperature.
clinicalTrials: Extensively used second-generation antihistamine with high safety profile and low sedation rates.

name: Sertraline (Zoloft)
description: Used to treat depression, panic attacks, obsessive-compulsive disorder, and social anxiety.
mechanismOfAction: Selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor (SSRI) that increases the levels of serotonin in the brain.

dosage.adults: Initially 50mg daily, may be increased up to 200mg daily.
dosage.children: Used for OCD in children 6-12 years: Initially 25mg daily.

precautions: Risk of suicidal thoughts in young adults. Do not stop suddenly. Avoid alcohol. Monitor for serotonin syndrome.
clinicalTrials: One of the most widely prescribed and studied antidepressants in the world.
